title: Implement and verify an RK4 integration step
description: Code the four-stage Runge-Kutta update and verify its fourth-order accuracy on a test problem.
body: Use when implementing a fixed-step RK4 integrator. Do not trust the implementation until its order is confirmed, since stage-weight mistakes are easy and silent. Actions: code the four stage evaluations and the weighted combination, run it on a problem with a known solution, and confirm error drops by sixteen each time the step halves. Validation signals include a measured fourth-order convergence rate and agreement with the analytical solution within tolerance. Risk boundary: an incorrect stage weighting degrades the order silently, so escalate to a convergence check whenever the integrator is modified.
